summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 9 warning, 13 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: ЁЯЯб bin/lib/gate.mjs:59 тАФ `cmdGate` still hard-codes `cwd: process.cwd()` instead of routing through the `--dir` argument it already resolves at line 17; literal grep audit flags this even though `_runSingleFeature` no longer routes through it. Backlog: change to `cwd: dir,` or delete if dead.
ЁЯЯб bin/lib/gate.mjs:59 тАФ Legacy `cmdGate` still passes `cwd: process.cwd()` to `execSync`; accept explicit `cwd` parameter or remove if dead code. File to backlog.
ЁЯЯб bin/lib/run.mjs:38 тАФ `harness()` wrapper uses `cwd: process.cwd()`; thread an explicit cwd through.
ЁЯФ┤ bin/lib/run.mjs:345 тАФ `dispatchToAgentAsync` has no `if (!cwd) throw` guard; `spawn({cwd: undefined})` silently inherits `process.cwd()`. Add the guard and a negative test.

ЁЯЯб bin/lib/gate.mjs:59 тАФ `execSync(cmd, { cwd: process.cwd() })` is a literal `process.cwd()` in a gate command in `bin/lib/`; would surface in any grep audit. Use `dir` (already accepted via `--dir`) instead.
ЁЯЯб bin/lib/run.mjs:373 тАФ `runParallelReviews` forwards `cwd` to `dispatchToAgentAsync` without validation; add an early guard.
ЁЯЯб test/worktree.test.mjs тАФ No automated grep-audit test. AC verification is manual and will rot. Add a test that scans `bin/lib/{run,gate}.mjs` for `process.cwd()` against an allowlist.
ЁЯЯб test/worktree.test.mjs тАФ Missing negative test for `dispatchToAgentAsync(_, _, undefined)` (twin of the existing `dispatchToAgent` case at line 298).

The ЁЯФ┤ was previously raised as ЁЯЯб in `task-3/eval-tester.md:45` and remains unfixed; given the AC explicitly covers "agent dispatch in `bin/lib/`", a silent-fallback sibling of `dispatchToAgent` is a critical gap.
ЁЯЯб bin/lib/run.mjs:345 тАФ `dispatchToAgentAsync` lacks the `if (!cwd) throw` guard that `dispatchToAgent` has; add for parity to fully close "no implicit fallback" on the parallel-review path

ЁЯЯб bin/lib/gate.mjs:59 тАФ `cwd: process.cwd()` is still present in the standalone `agt gate` execSync. Outside task-3's stated scope (which only targeted `runGateInline` + `dispatchToAgent`), but the broader feature claim "no gate command in `bin/lib/` references `process.cwd()` directly" is, read literally, contradicted here. From a pure simplicity lens there is no over-engineering тАФ flagging only because the audit claim and the grep do not fully agree; correctness verification belongs to architect/tester.
